---
title: "Automatic Differentiation and Differentiable Programming"
subtitle: "Track D — Scientific ML"
date: "2025-01-01"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
number-sections: true
---

# Automatic Differentiation and Differentiable Programming

**Track**: Track D — Scientific ML

Automatic differentiation is the computational engine powering modern scientific machine learning. This textbook covers the theory, implementation, and application of AD to scientific computing.

## Table of Contents

1. Principles of Automatic Differentiation
2. Forward-Mode AD in Depth
3. Reverse-Mode AD in Depth
4. AD Through Differential Equation Solvers
5. AD Through Linear Algebra
6. Differentiable Programming Paradigm
7. Second-Order Methods and Hessians
8. Advanced Topics: Custom Rules, Sparsity, and Mixed Precision

---

## Chapter 1: Principles of Automatic Differentiation

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 2: Forward-Mode AD in Depth

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 3: Reverse-Mode AD in Depth

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 4: AD Through Differential Equation Solvers

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 5: AD Through Linear Algebra

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 6: Differentiable Programming Paradigm

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 7: Second-Order Methods and Hessians

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 8: Advanced Topics: Custom Rules, Sparsity, and Mixed Precision

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*
